title: Data Importer with Enable  Banking.  Fatal error: file_get_contents(): errno=21 Is a directory in SecretManager.php due to missing is_file check
author: oleksiips
body:
### Support guidelines

- [x] I'm smart and I read the <!-- MZ2udTpin6FL --> [support guidelines](https://github.com/firefly-iii/firefly-iii/blob/main/.github/support.md)

### Use of AI

- [x] I'm a real person and wrote this bug without assistance from AI.

### I've found a bug and checked that ...

- [x] ... [the documentation](https://docs.firefly-iii.org/) does not mention anything about my problem
- [x] ... there are no open or closed issues that are related to my problem
- [x] ... it's [definitely a Firefly III issue, not me](https://github.com/firefly-iii/firefly-iii/blob/main/.github/its_you_not_me.md)

### Description

In Docker/Kubernetes environments (like TrueNAS SCALE), volume mounts can sometimes create empty directories instead of files if the source path is misconfigured or cached during deployment.

Currently, in app/Services/EnableBanking/Authentication/SecretManager.php (around line 99), the condition checks if the private key can be read:
// see if this is a file that exists and is readable.
if(false !== realpath($privateKey) &&
   file_exists(realpath($privateKey)) && is_readable(realpath($privateKey))) {
    Log::debug('Enable Banking private key is a file, will try to read it.');
    $privateKey = file_get_contents(realpath($privateKey));
}

- [x] ... it's [definitely a Firefly III issue, not me](https://github.com/firefly-iii/firefly-iii/blob/main/.github/its_you_not_me.md)

### Description

In Docker/Kubernetes environments (like TrueNAS SCALE), volume mounts can sometimes create empty directories instead of files if the source path is misconfigured or cached during deployment.

Currently, in app/Services/EnableBanking/Authentication/SecretManager.php (around line 99), the condition checks if the private key can be read:
// see if this is a file that exists and is readable.
if(false !== realpath($privateKey) &&
   file_exists(realpath($privateKey)) && is_readable(realpath($privateKey))) {
    Log::debug('Enable Banking private key is a file, will try to read it.');
    $privateKey = file_get_contents(realpath($privateKey));
}

### Debug information

In PHP, both file_exists() and is_readable() return true for directories. If the Docker environment accidentally mounts the $privateKey path as a directory, the condition passes, and file_get_contents() attempts to read a directory.

This results in a fatal crash:

### Description

I believe it is common to pay for a bill and later receive payments from your friends. That's probably why transaction links is here to link a transaction is partially paid by another transaction. Currently, the budget will be calculated by the expense without considering the bill may be shared among others. While temporarily increasing the budget for that month with reference to the money received from friends is a work around, this should be done by the system utilizing transaction links.

### Solution

Periodic budget should consider the income that may be linked to other expenses that pays (partially) for that transaction. When it is related to a budget, the income amount should be used to increase that month's budget or decrease that month's total expense within the budget. Please let me know what you think.

title: native_amount conversion uses now() instead of transaction date
author: tarzan77cz
body:
### Support guidelines

- [x] I've read the [support guidelines](https://github.com/firefly-iii/firefly-iii/blob/main/.github/support.md)

### I've found a bug and checked that ...

- [x] ... [the documentation](https://docs.firefly-iii.org/) does not mention anything about my problem
- [x] ... there are no open or closed issues that are related to my problem
- [x] ... it's [definitely a Firefly III issue, not me](https://github.com/firefly-iii/firefly-iii/blob/main/.github/its_you_not_me.md)

### Description

When `convert_to_primary` is enabled, `native_amount` on transactions is recalculated using the exchange rate for **today** (`now()`), not the **transaction journal date**.

`TransactionObserver` correctly sets `$params->date = $transaction->transactionJournal->date`, but `ConvertsAmountToPrimaryAmount::convert()` ignores it:

https://github.com/firefly-iii/firefly-iii/blob/main/app/Handlers/ExchangeRate/ConvertsAmountToPrimaryAmount.php

```php
$newAmount = $converter->convert($params->originalCurrency, $primaryCurrency, now(), $amount);
```

`ExchangeRateConverter` is designed for historical lookups (`date <= $date ORDER BY date DESC`), and `ConversionParameters` has a `$date` field for this purpose.

### Expected behaviour

`native_amount` should use the exchange rate valid on the transaction's journal date (`$params->date`).

### Actual behaviour

All conversions use the rate at conversion time. Running `php artisan correction:recalculate-pc-amounts` resets every `native_amount` and recalculates them all with the **same current rate**, regardless of transaction date.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable "Convert amounts to your primary currency" in Preferences.
2. Ensure historical exchange rates exist (e.g. EUR→CZK for multiple dates).
3. Have EUR transactions from different dates (e.g. interest deposits in 2025).
4. Run `php artisan correction:recalculate-pc-amounts`.
5. Compare `native_amount / amount` for transactions on different dates — all use the same rate (rate for `now()`).

### Environment

- Firefly III version: **6.6.6**
- PHP: 8.x (Docker image)

### Suggested fix

Replace `now()` with `$params->date` in `ConvertsAmountToPrimaryAmount::convert()`.

title: Feature request: allow linking two Transfer transactions together
author: mircea-pavel-anton
body:
## Is your feature request related to a problem?

Transaction links (Paid, Refund, Reimbursement, Related) currently seem to work between a Transfer and a Withdrawal/Deposit, but not between two Transfer transactions. In the web UI, attempting to link two transfers to each other results in "Error! Cannot link these transactions."

## Use case

I track a "self-loan" pattern: when I run short on money in my checking account toward the end of the month, I move funds from my savings account into checking to cover the gap (a Transfer), and then pay it back into savings once my salary arrives the following month (another Transfer). Both legs are Transfers between my own asset accounts. No expense or income is involved, so there's nothing to categorize as a Withdrawal/Deposit.

Sometimes this isn't a single withdrawal/repayment pair either. It can be several smaller Transfers out of savings over the course of a month (covering different shortfalls), all settled by one larger Transfer back into savings once income arrives. Right now I have no way to link the repayment Transfer to the Transfers it's settling, since linking two Transfers isn't allowed.

## Describe the solution you'd like

Allow the existing Transaction Link feature (in particular the "Paid"/"Reimbursement" link types) to be used between two transactions of any kind, the same way it currently works between a Withdrawal and a Deposit. Ideally also support linking one outward Transfer (the repayment) to multiple inward Transfers (the individual withdrawals it settles), since a single repayment may cover several earlier advances.

## Describe alternatives you've considered

Currently working around this by applying a shared tag (e.g. `self-loan`) to all the related Transfer transactions, which lets me pull them up together, but doesn't give the explicit "this repayment settles that withdrawal" relationship that Transaction Links provide for expenses.

author: Weenig
body:

- [x] ... it's [definitely a Firefly III issue, not me](https://github.com/firefly-iii/firefly-iii/blob/main/.github/its_you_not_me.md)

### Description

On very large imports the way the import submissions is saved, the file grows and slows down the import process from 1.9k the first hr to 600 the 3rd hr. 

### Debug information

N/A

### Steps to reproduce

### Additional info

Local fix: save every 100 transactions plus once after the loop; the same import then finished in under an hour.

title: Import job file is written non-atomically; status endpoint intermittently fails with "The file for import job X is empty
author: Weenig
body:
### Support guidelines

- [x] I'm smart and I read the <!-- MZ2udTpin6FL --> [support guidelines](https://github.com/firefly-iii/firefly-iii/blob/main/.github/support.md)

### Use of AI

- [x] I'm a real person and wrote this bug without assistance from AI.

### I've found a bug and checked that ...

- [x] ... [the documentation](https://docs.firefly-iii.org/) does not mention anything about my problem
- [x] ... there are no open or closed issues that are related to my problem
- [x] ... it's [definitely a Firefly III issue, not me](https://github.com/firefly-iii/firefly-iii/blob/main/.github/its_you_not_me.md)

### Description

During the import of 10000+ bank transfers, the importer imtermittenly showed an error, even though the importer was working fine. 
Import was done with Enable Banking. 
A potential cause is that saveToDisk() rewrites the job JSON with a plain $disk->put() (truncate-then-write). The file was 12 MB; the status endpoint polls every second and reads the file mid-truncate.

### Debug information

Log shows ImporterErrorException "The file for import job ... is empty" from
ImportJobRepository::find() (line ~86), thrown ~2,000 times over one import.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run an import large enough that the job file grows to several MB (10k
   transactions did it).
2. Keep the submission status page open (it polls /status every second).
3. Watch the log: intermittent "The file for import job X is empty" exceptions;
   the UI shows the submission as failed although it continues.

### Additional info

Local fix that eliminated it completely: write to a temp file, then rename (atomic on POSIX) in saveToDisk(); plus a short retry-on-empty in find().

### Description

The current implantation of exchange rate, according to the documentation, only support **weekly** update for the 38 built-in currencies. Which is fine for most of the people, but I think could be improved.

### Solution

I propose adding support to the [Frankfurter API](https://frankfurter.dev/) for exchange rates data. Not only it support **daily** exchange rates for over 200 currencies, it's also FOSS and self-hostable.

The app could kept the base currencies unchanged. For people who add additional currencies, those can be easily support auto update of exchange rates when they set the currency codes correctly.
